description: CRIT-801 maps EC-IPC-013 (whitespace-only body) to `prop_ipc_017 (relies on body.is_empty() byte-level check)` at editor_wire_sprint8.rs:64. Inspection of `prop_ipc_017_compose_select_past_note` (lines 1041-1071) shows it tests exactly two body inputs: `"# Hello"` (line 1043) and `""` (line 1064). It NEVER constructs a whitespace-only body input like `"   "`, `"\n\n"`, or `"\t"`. The map's hand-wave 'relies on body.is_empty() byte-level check' is a code-inference claim, not a test assertion. Critically, `body.is_empty()` returns FALSE for whitespace strings (e.g., `"   ".is_empty() == false`), so the implementation classifies a whitespace-only body as `is_note_empty: false` — and no test currently verifies whether this is the spec-correct behavior for EC-IPC-013. The edge case is mechanically counted by the wc -l == 14 regex but semantically uncovered. Phase 5 graders following the map will discover that EC-IPC-013's behavior is implementation-defined, not test-anchored. This is a softer version of FIND-091: the structural map passes the regex but a real reviewer must read the underlying test and discover the gap.

evidence.snippet: ///   EC-IPC-013 (whitespace-only body)        — prop_ipc_017 (relies on body.is_empty() byte-level check)

remediationOptions: Extend prop_ipc_017_compose_select_past_note with a third input case `compose_state_for_select_past_note("note-3", "   ")` and explicitly assert is_note_empty == false (matching the spec EC-IPC-013 expected behavior, whatever that is).

If the spec EC-IPC-013 actually says whitespace-only should be treated as empty, then add a trim-or-equivalent step in compose_state_for_select_past_note AND add the assertion is_note_empty == true for the whitespace-only input. Either way, anchor the EC in a real assertion.
Update the coverage map comment from 'relies on body.is_empty() byte-level check' (which describes implementation behavior) to a test name that DOES assert the EC-IPC-013 behavior.
